question_structure.context: A point charge with a charge of \( q = -3 \times 10^{-6} \, \mathrm{C} \) is moved from point A to point B in an electric field. During this movement, the charge overcomes the electric field force, performing work of \( 6 \times 10^{-4} \, \mathrm{J} \). Subsequently, when the charge is moved from point B to point C, the electric field force does work of \( -9 \times 10^{-4} \, \mathrm{J} \) on the charge.
question_structure.sub_question_1: What are the potential differences between AB, BC, and CA?
question_structure.sub_question_2: If the potential at point B is zero, what are the potentials at points A and C? What are the potential energies of the charge at points A and C?

sub_question_1.step_1: The potential difference between points A and B is $U_{AB} = \frac{W_{AB}}{q} = \frac{-6 \times 10^{-4}}{-3 \times 10^{-6}} \mathrm{V} = 200 \mathrm{V}$.
sub_question_1.step_2: The potential difference between points B and C is $U_{BC} = \frac{W_{BC}}{q} = \frac{-9 \times 10^{-4}}{-3 \times 10^{-6}} \mathrm{V} = 300 \mathrm{V}$.
sub_question_1.step_3: The potential difference between points A and C is $U_{AC} = U_{AB} + U_{BC} = 200 + 300 = 500 \mathrm{V}$.

sub_question_2.step_5: The electric potential at point C is $\varphi_{C} = \varphi_{B} - U_{BC} = 0 - 300 \mathrm{V} = -300 \mathrm{V}$.
sub_question_2.step_6: The electric potential energy at point A is $E_{PA} = q \varphi_{A} = -3 \times 10^{-6} \times 200 \mathrm{J} = -6 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{J}$.
sub_question_2.step_7: The electric potential energy at point C is $E_{PC} = q \varphi_{C} = -3 \times 10^{-6} \times (-300) \mathrm{J} = 9 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{J}$.

step_1.physical_theorem: Definition of potential difference (work done by electric force)

result_quantity.name: Potential difference between points A and B

step_2.physical_theorem: Definition of potential difference (work done by electric force)

result_quantity.name: Potential difference between points B and C
Potential difference between points A and C

step_4.physical_theorem: Relationship between potential difference and electric potential

Electric potential energy of the charge at point C
